label: Sparse coding & compositional representations

node.label: Sparse coding & compositional representations
node.definition: Information is represented by a small active subset of features, improving compression, interpretability, or conditional computation.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Sparse coding & compositional representations is modeled as depending on Embeddings & distributed representations, Information theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
examples: atlas downstream link to Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis
limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Embeddings & distributed representations, Information theory.

dependencies.rationale: Sparse coding & compositional representations consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
In this atlas graph, Sparse coding & compositional representations has a `requires` dependency on Information theory with `core` strength.
Sparse coding & compositional representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Optimization theory.
